roofline_perfect_retile_ceiling.label: Roofline perfect-f->1 re-tile ceiling on the int4-GEMM (greedy-UNSAFE)

closed_levers.lever: pinned-K self-referential Triton split-K verify GEMM
closed_levers.mechanism: Pin the K-split of the verify GEMM to a self-referential canonical layout to shave verify time (modeled +13.998 -> 496.74 rung).
closed_levers.reason: Buildable on the served Triton split-K kernel, but the modeled +13.998 inverts to -5.82 realized. 496.74 REFUTED as a realizable speed rung. First confirmed modeled-in-isolation collapse this cycle.

closed_levers.mechanism: Realize the cb3 sub-int4 weight byte-saving on the served kernel to shrink the verify HBM read (modeled +15.60 -> 482.74).
closed_levers.reason: The sub-int4 RHT+VQ byte-saving is un-consumable without a bf16 materialize (penalty_materialize=0.065, ~15x inversion). 482.74 collapses to the 467.14 base, BELOW the deployed 481.53. cb3 is LEGAL-but-not-FAST; the ~520.5 fused counterfactual needs a human-gated kernel build (see supply-fused-kernel).

closed_levers.lever: Fused RHT+VQ sub-int4 decode kernel (from-source build prize)
closed_levers.mechanism: Build the human-gated fused RHT+VQ sub-int4 sm_86 decode kernel that would let cb3's byte-saving turn into BW saving (idealized roofline 521.82).
closed_levers.reason: Achievable on Ampere primitives (FLUTE/AQLM/QuIP# existence proofs, cp.async+mma.sync; no Hopper wgmma/TMA), but charging the in-GEMM codebook-gather (gamma central 0.85) + online-FWHT (phi central 0.025) tax honestly collapses the idealized 521.82 roofline to a realistic 475.86. ~12-20 expert-CUDA person-weeks; only the optimistic FLUTE-parity gamma>=0.93 corner (501.45) clears 481, fragile within-noise. Same BW-surrogate optimism as pinned-K/cb3.

closed_levers.mechanism: Find a byte-exact tile/config that speeds the dominant int4-Marlin W4A16 verify GEMM (85% of verify).
closed_levers.reason: Marlin is the UNIQUE int4 GEMM on sm_86 (Machete/Cutlass=Hopper cap-90 confirmed via machete_supported_schedules; AllSpark=no-g128; Exllama=fp16). It exposes NO Python tile knob (auto in-kernel). The lone use_fp32_reduce=False knob breaks byte-exactness on 3/4 shapes (split-K reorder, max|delta|<=3.9e-3); identity-breaking upper bound is +0.64 < +2. A faster GEMM is a source build (cf supply-fused-kernel). Isolated GEMM ~3931us @ 92% peak HBM.

closed_levers.lever: Drafter Triton-kernel tile sweep (fused sparse argmax)
closed_levers.mechanism: Re-tile the drafter's only Triton-tunable kernel (the fused sparse argmax, 6.52% of D) to shrink draft time D.
closed_levers.reason: The served default (BLOCK_SELECTED=16, num_warps=8) WINS the full 45-config grid at sub-us precision; every alternative is equal or slower. The other ~93% of D is int4-Marlin GEMM (supply-int4-gemm-marlin's domain). Even the unreachable ceiling (argmax cost -> 0) is only +5.71 TPS. Cleanest realized-NULL: the microbench delta is itself zero.

closed_levers.mechanism: Re-tile the only tunable Triton kernel in verify (the 7-layer Triton-3D split-KV global attention, 1.27% of verify) -- the end-to-end adjudication of wirbel #442's modeled +15.86.
closed_levers.reason: Verify wall = 85.09% vendored int4-Marlin GEMM (no Triton tile surface) + 14.19% attention, of which only ~9% (1.27% of verify, 75.3us) is the tunable Triton-3D kernel. num_stages 3->2 is a REAL, reproducible +6.11% on that kernel (sigma~0.01us), but mapped honestly through the cycle fraction it is +0.2613 TPS end-to-end. Even DELETING the whole kernel caps at +4.27. #442's modeled +15.86 back-implies a 279.9us saving = 3.7x the entire tunable kernel -> physically impossible from a tile retune.

closed_levers.mechanism: Serve weights below int4 to cut the HBM-bound verify read directly.
closed_levers.reason: No native sub-int4 GEMM path exists on Ampere sm_86 under the served vLLM 0.22 stack. The only realization route is the human-gated fused RHT+VQ kernel build, itself sized NO-GO at central (supply-fused-kernel).

closed_levers.mechanism: Exploit M-scaling of the verify attention to pick a faster speculative width K (the single primary untested TPS-model assumption).
closed_levers.reason: t_attn_frac_of_verify=0.0690 (attention is <7% of verify) and the M-scaling Triton attention is M-FLAT (T_attn M=8/M=4 ratio 1.005). k_opt=7 equals the deployed K -> kopt_lift_tps=0.0. Verify is GEMM-dominated (M-invariant int4-Marlin); attention is too small a slice to retarget. THE single primary untested TPS-model assumption, now MEASURED null.

closed_levers.lever: Static CUDA-graph capture of the K=7/M=8 spec-decode loop
closed_levers.mechanism: Capture the spec-decode loop as a CUDA graph to remove host launch overhead.
closed_levers.reason: The K=7/M=8 loop is ALREADY replayed as one CUDA graph (ONEGRAPH=1, PR #75; paged-KV block-table refresh correctly stays outside). host_overhead_frac_graph_on=0.004555 < 0.5% gate -> no host gap to recover. Bonus A/B: the deployed loop-graph already banks 67us/step (forcing graphs OFF would cost -3.93 TPS on the frontier).

closed_levers.mechanism: Overlap the drafter forward Dn with the prior verify Vn-1 on a second CUDA stream to hide D (D/V=0.222, an 18.2% theoretical prize).
closed_levers.reason: The 18.2% overlap prize is real, but the drafter consumes the prior pass's VERIFIED hidden state, pinning the critical path to the serial chain V(n-1)->Dn->Vn. The PR's own stream_B.wait_event(verify_done) IS that serialization: byte-exact <=> wait_event <=> no overlap. Overlap requires a contaminated not-yet-verified state -> breaks greedy identity. Same realized-NULL class as cb3/pinned-K.

closed_levers.lever: Async KV-cache L2 prefetch for verify attention
closed_levers.mechanism: Prefetch KV into L2 ahead of the verify-attention read to hide KV-read latency.
closed_levers.reason: Attention is 0.0928 of verify (< the 10% self-abort gate); the served Triton verify-attention kernel ALREADY emits cp.async pipelining (93 ops); and the A10G L2 is 6.29MB, not the 40MB the hypothesis assumed (A100-spec error caught) -> prefetch delta ~ 0. Correct self-abort.

closed_levers.mechanism: Retrain the MTP K=7 drafter (DeepSeek-MTP-KL-distill alpha=0.5) to recover the position-1 near-miss pool WITHOUT changing topology.
closed_levers.reason: pos1_fixed_capacity_recoverable_frac=0.0 -- the binding position-1 miss yields no lift even under the prescribed recipe (accept-len 3.83 ~ baseline 3.844). The 65.3% near-miss pool is recoverable only by a BIGGER drafter (SIZE), forbidden by no-topology. Even optimistic full-delivery 482.50 = +0.97 over 481.53, inside sigma_hw~4.8. The DEMAND axis closes (joins supply).

closed_levers.lever: Bigger drafter at ANY size (net-TPS oracle, topology FREE)
closed_levers.mechanism: Allow the drafter capacity multiplier s to grow; model TPS_net(s) = demand_gain(phi(s)) x supply(D(s)) charging the added drafter latency D(s).
closed_levers.reason: Even with topology FREE, no defensible drafter-size net-beats 481.53. D(s) charges ~16.9% TPS per unit s at s=1, while breakeven needs phi'(1) > 0.934 (recover >93% of the near-miss pool per unit s). Literature capacity->acceptance beta_alpha~0.02 (band [0.007,0.053]) gives a 2x drafter -> 1.4% of pool, 4x -> 2.7%. Critical beta to beat 481 is 2.78 ~ 139x the central literature exponent -- physically incredible. Even a perfect oracle (phi=1) fails by s=2 (471.9 < 481.53). Demand axis closed ON NET.

closed_levers.mechanism: Verify a full token tree per step to accept more tokens per verify.
closed_levers.reason: Full-fanout tree verify nets strongly negative: the extra verify cost of the wide tree dwarfs the marginal accepted tokens at bs=1 on the A10G.

closed_levers.mechanism: Use dynamic programming to pick a per-position tree width between full-fanout and the linear chain.
closed_levers.reason: The best interior per-position-DP shape nets only +1.33 TPS and is beta-fragile -- it does not survive acceptance-rate uncertainty, so it is not a robust lever above the deployed number.

closed_levers.mechanism: Swap the verify-attention backend to FlashInfer for a faster paged-attention kernel.
closed_levers.reason: vLLM force-pins TRITON_ATTN for Gemma-4's heterogeneous head dims (sliding head_dim 256 / global 512); the FlashInfer backend cannot be selected for this model under the served stack.

closed_levers.mechanism: Augment speculative decode with n-gram/prompt-lookup draft proposals to win extra accepts for free.
closed_levers.closing_number_text: lane closed (PLD hits redundant with MTP accept)
closed_levers.reason: Prompt-lookup hits correlate with what the MTP drafter already accepts (redundant); realized lift is small (~+1.67%) and P(hit | drafter-miss) ~ 0.035, so the n-gram lane adds no net token throughput over the deployed MTP drafter.

closed_levers.mechanism: Vary the speculative width K online (per-step accept-history) instead of a static K.
closed_levers.reason: No online signal beats a static K: the adaptive-K control is unrealizable (the oracle lift does not survive the realizable online estimator), so the lever collapses to static-K -- which is then measured (fresh-static-k).

closed_levers.mechanism: Pick a different fixed speculative width K to trade draft cost against accept length.
closed_levers.reason: Static K=4 measured -8.63% vs K=7 on the served benchmark -> K=7 is the optimum; no static-K change helps. The earlier modeled adaptive-K gains (+13.2% / +4.28%) collapse to a measured -8.63% (an isolation-collapse instance).

closed_levers.mechanism: Fuse the entire decode step (GEMMs + attention + norms + sampling) into one persistent megakernel to kill dispatch/fusion overhead.
closed_levers.reason: A fully-fused megakernel is a from-source CUDA build (same family as supply-fused-kernel), not a config lever. The residual verify wall after the int4-GEMM (#448 finding) is fusion/dispatch -- recoverable only by a build, which is NO-GO without human-gated kernel work.

reconciliation.title: The ~16% int4-GEMM achieved-BW headroom is REAL but GREEDY-UNSAFE
reconciliation.pr447_says: +15.86 is IMPOSSIBLE from a tile retune. #447 measures the only tunable Triton kernel in verify -- the 7-layer Triton-3D split-KV attention at 75.3us = 1.27% of verify. A real +6.11% retune of it is +0.26 TPS end-to-end; even DELETING the whole kernel caps at +4.27. #442's +15.86 back-implies a 279.9us saving = 3.7x the entire tunable kernel.
reconciliation.pr450_says: +15.86 is PHYSICALLY PLAUSIBLE. #450 measures the aggregate vendored int4-Marlin GEMM (85% of verify). It runs at 433 GB/s = 84% of measured read-peak (518 GB/s) -- a real ~16% BW headroom. A perfect f->1 re-tile reaches 510.87 TPS (clears 481.53 by +29.3), and the 467.14->481.53 gap (14.39) is INSIDE that headroom.
reconciliation.why_both_correct: They speak to DIFFERENT kernels. #447's '+15.86 impossible from a tile retune' is about the tunable Triton ATTENTION kernel (1.27% of verify, byte-exact retune -> +0.26). #450's '+15.86 physically plausible' is about the dominant vendored int4-Marlin GEMM (85% of verify). The GEMM's ~16% BW headroom is real, but Marlin exposes NO Python tile knob (#448); the ONLY way to recover it is FP-reassociating split-K / BLOCK_K / num_warps re-tiling, which reorders the int4 accumulation -> byte-divergent -> GREEDY-UNSAFE. #450's realistic split-K recovers only +12.6..+31.4 TPS and is flagged realistic_splitk_greedy_safe=false.
reconciliation.decision_consequence: The strict frontier is genuinely closed: every byte-exact lever realizes <= noise (max +0.26, #447). The only material headroom is the int4-GEMM BW slack, recoverable only by greedy-UNSAFE FP-reassociation -- exactly the non-equivalence the prize requires relaxing. Roofline physics does NOT cap the sweep below 481.53; greedy-safety is the binding constraint. This is why the relax-strict-equivalence question (#407) must go to the human.

isolation_collapses.lesson: Always realize end-to-end; never report the isolated-op delta. Each lever below reported a large modeled isolated-op gain that collapsed or inverted when mapped through the realized end-to-end cycle. This is exactly why the relax-prize cards (stark/ubel) must be MEASURED, not modeled.
